RPMac - Quick start
===================

1. Keep RPMac.exe, smccore.exe and inpout32.dll TOGETHER in the same folder.
2. Right-click RPMac.exe  ->  "Run as administrator"
   (administrator rights are required to access the Mac's hardware/SMC).
3. Set each fan to Auto / Max / a custom RPM, or use "Curve" to ramp the fan
   between a min and max temperature. Temperatures update live.

Scripting (optional)
--------------------
smccore.exe is a command-line tool for the same control, e.g.:
   smccore.exe list                 show fans
   smccore.exe set 0 2000           fan 0 -> 2000 RPM
   smccore.exe auto 0               fan 0 -> automatic
   smccore.exe max                  all fans -> maximum
Run it from an administrator terminal, with inpout32.dll in the folder.

Requirements
------------
- An Intel Mac running Windows (Boot Camp).
- On non-Apple PCs the app stays READ-ONLY and changes nothing.

Antivirus note
--------------
RPMac uses InpOut32, a low-level I/O driver, to talk to the Mac's SMC
(the chip that controls the fans). Some antivirus tools flag this kind of
driver as "potentially unwanted" because it grants hardware access.
This is normal for fan-control utilities. RPMac is fully open source:
https://github.com/golirt1/RPMAC
If your antivirus blocks it, allow it manually.

Safety
------
Running the fan at maximum is safe for the hardware, but constant max speed
means more noise and wear over time. RPMac never disables the SMC's built-in
thermal protection.
